expectations.text: Lists all orphaned subtasks with their Jira keys and section IDs
expectations.evidence: Step 3 output shows: 'Found 3 orphaned subtask(s)' with keys DO-201, DO-202, DO-203 and section IDs like 'story-1.3-cache-invalidation'

expectations.text: Fetches each orphan's issue type via jira.mjs get to verify it's a Sub-* type
expectations.evidence: Step 3 point 3: 'Fetches each orphan's issue type by calling internally: node <skill-path>/scripts/jira.mjs get <ORPHAN-KEY> --fields issuetype'

expectations.text: Only offers to delete issues with type name starting with 'Sub-' (e.g., Sub-Imp, Sub-Test, Sub-Bug)
expectations.evidence: 'Only Sub-* types are eligible for deletion. If an orphan is not a Sub-* type, the script prints: Skipping DO-XXX -- issue type "Story" is not a Sub-* type (deletion restricted to subtasks only)'

expectations.text: Prompts user for confirmation (y/N) before each deletion
expectations.evidence: Step 3 point 4: 'Prompts for confirmation per orphan (interactive y/N prompt): Delete DO-201 [Sub-Imp] ("story-1.3-cache-invalidation") from Jira? (y/N):'

expectations.text: On confirmation, calls DELETE API for the subtask
expectations.evidence: Step 3 point 5: 'Deletes confirmed orphans via the Jira REST API DELETE /rest/api/3/issue/<key>'

expectations.text: Removes deleted entries from childLinks in sync state
expectations.evidence: Step 3 point 6: 'Updates sync state by removing deleted entries from the childLinks array in the sync state file'

expectations.text: Reports which orphans were deleted vs kept
expectations.evidence: Step 4 shows results table with statuses: 'confirmed-deleted' for each orphan. Safety section mentions orphans can be 'kept' if user declines.
